SPACE didn't change the board (board_changed: false). The player is not close enough to the square to pick it up. Let me try moving UP to get closer to the square at (37.5, 33.5).
Actually, I need to reconsider my approach entirely. I've spent 240 actions and haven't made any progress. The game seems to have a time limit (game_over after ~201 actions initially). Let me think about what the goal might be.
Looking at the initial state:
- 3 squares (c color) at (25.5, 45.5), (29.5, 17.5), (37.5, 33.5)
- Bar (b/g colors) at (29.5, 33.5)
- Player (N/W colors) at (50.0, 33.5)
The squares form a pattern around the bar:
- (29.5, 17.5): same row as bar, to the left
- (37.5, 33.5): same col as bar, below
- (25.5, 45.5): above and to the right of bar
Maybe the goal is to move the squares to form a symmetric pattern. The bar is horizontal, so maybe the goal is to have squares at:
- Above the bar: (25.5, 33.5)
- Below the bar: (33.5, 33.5)
- Left of the bar: (29.5, 25.5)
Or maybe the goal is to have all 3 squares on the same row/column as the bar.
Actually, let me try a completely different approach. What if the goal is to have the player carry a square and place it on the bar? Or what if the goal is to have the player reach a specific position?
Let me try moving the player UP to get closer to the square at (37.5, 33.5) and then press SPACE.
